title: Security policy
text: report vulnerability public issue, discussion, test fixture, request log. Use GitHub’s private vulnerability reporting. form unavailable, contact repository owner using private contact method listed ksonda GitHub profile. Include, possible: affected version commit; minimal reproduction expose third-party data; impact realistic attack conditions; suggested mitigations; whether issue disclosed elsewhere. maintainer aims acknowledge report within three business days provide initial assessment within seven. Remediation disclosure timing depend severity, exploitability, upstream coordination. Credit offered unless reporter prefers anonymity.

title: Security policy
text: Reports especially useful weaknesses involving: server-side request forgery provider-controlled URLs redirects; access loopback, private, link-local, cloud-metadata addresses; unsafe remote JSON-LD contexts; decompression bombs, parser-depth exhaustion, budget bypasses; cache poisoning cross-representation cache collisions; SPARQL injection unsafe query-template substitution; unintended code execution classifying fetching distribution; credentials sensitive values exposed diagnostics request ledgers. Service outages, ordinary upstream schema drift, public-data corrections usually reliability issues rather vulnerabilities. Report bug template unless create security impact.

Several supposedly verified assumptions already inconsistent live behavior.

what: 1. The AOI query has a silent GeoSPARQL bug
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: spec names gsp:sfIntersects, GeoSPARQL query functions use geof:sfIntersects http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/. incorrect form can return empty successful result, worse explicit error makes catalog appear complete. fallback reference collections semantically equivalent: can see reference gages monitoring locations represented graph. Resolution v0.2: correct namespace; live anchored test; incomplete spatial fallback must reported partial, never passed complete.

what: 2. The parser contract does not match live JSON-LD
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: checked reference-gage document contains literal hyf:HY_HydroLocationType, provider IRI string, nested hyf:HY_IndirectPosition, WKT @value object. also contains gsp: key declaring geo: context. Local prefix expansion aimed one ideal shape robust enough. Resolution v0.2: standards-based expansion bundled/allowlisted contexts, tolerant Geoconnex-profile extractor, structured diagnostics unsupported malformed variants.

what: 3. A verified PID currently lands on a failing item route
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: PID mainstem 29559 returned expected 303, direct mainstems/items/29559 route returned HTTP 500 review. filtered collection query still returned item. Resolution v0.2: item → filtered collection → negotiated JSON-LD fallback, large-mainstem fixture. mainstems/v3 decision becomes P0 gate.

what: 4. The dataflow and naming contradict the stated product boundary
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: “Every exported function returns tibble sf” conflicts client, list, S3 orchestration, path, text returns. gx_twin(aoi, dir, ...) later called undeclared recipe=. gx_package(fetched) accept explicit harmonized stage explain catalog-packaging. non-goal forbids “twin” language flagship API report use . Resolution v0.2: documented S3 objects; coherent catalog→plan→fetch→harmonize→package flow; gx_snapshot(); separate gx_replay().

what: 5. The frozen dataset ID can collide
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: original row represents site × dataset × distribution, potentially multiple variables, dataset_id hashes site, name, distribution URL. conflates dataset distribution identity can collide across variables. Resolution v0.2: separate dataset, distribution, variable identifiers; explicit row cardinality; cross-language SHA-256 canonicalization; contracts remain experimental spike provider audit.

title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: Appending LIMIT/OFFSET arbitrary query invalid meaning-changing ASK, CONSTRUCT, DESCRIBE, aggregates, nested queries, existing limits. Without stable ordering, OFFSET pagination can omit duplicate mutable results. signature also omits page argument described prose. Resolution v0.2: raw SPARQL executes ; named SELECT templates declared order, result key, budget paginate.

what: 7. Replay was incorrectly described as reproducibility
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: future run changing services guarantee identical row counts. manifest omitted query/registry/vocabulary hashes, response asset hashes, HTTP validators, custom AOI geometry, custom-handler replayability. Resolution v0.2: distinguish recipe replay offline snapshot verification; preserve request ledger SHA-256 resource hashes; remove identical-live-row-count claims.

what: 8. The handler interface was classification, not an executable protocol
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: EDR URL enough decide collection, query verb, AOI/location, parameter, time, response format. URL matching also define request/byte/page budgets normalized payload contracts. Runtime R package names supposedly language-neutral YAML weaken Python-port claim. Resolution v0.2: probe → plan → fetch → normalize; portable classifiers separated language-specific implementations; visible, deterministic fetch plan.

what: 9. Provider URLs create an unaddressed SSRF boundary
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: Crawled contentUrl values redirects can point local/private/link-local/cloud-metadata destinations oversized/compressed payloads. CSV content-type checking alone insufficient. Resolution v0.2: HTTP(S)-policy, address redirect revalidation, encoded/decoded byte budgets, parser limits, explicit trusted-private override policy.

what: 10. The unit-conversion model is mathematically wrong
title: Review of geoconnexr specification v0.1
text: spec calls conversions multiplicative includes Fahrenheit↔︎Celsius. Temperature conversions require scale offset. Resolution v0.2: directed reviewed rules using converted = original * scale + offset, canonical unit URIs, dimensional checks, raw-value preservation.

what: M1 — Request, cache, and safety core
title: geoconnexr — validated specification and build roadmap
text: Internal execution supports method, canonical URL, headers, body, endpoint policy, timeout, retry, throttle, redirect policy, maximum encoded/decoded bytes, offline mode. Requirements: retry 429 selected 5xx/transport failures, honor Retry-, use jitter; every physical attempt must revalidate DNS consume aggregate request/byte budgets; throttle parallelize per host; PID/JSON-LD maximum active requests default four; graph queries POST-reject redirects HTML responses; cache key includes method, canonical URL, body hash, Accept, Content-Type, endpoint base, cache schema version; cache entries record response status, relevant headers, retrieval time, final URL, SHA-256; never cache error/partial responses successes; use atomic writes recover corruption; store tools::R_user_dir("geoconnexr", "cache") default; offline=TRUE permits valid cached responses reports cache misses clearly; provider-controlled URLs, allow HTTP(S) ; reject loopback, private, link-local, cloud-metadata targets default re-check every redirect. httr2::req_cache() may used compliant GETs, POST SPARQL caching needs package cache req_cache() cache POST requests. Acceptance: deterministic tests injected clock/jitter cover retry, throttle, representation-specific cache keys, expiry, offline misses, concurrent writes, corrupt entries, redirect revalidation, decompression ceiling, graph HTML errors. lint rule forbids direct httr2::request() outside L1, excluding documented external-package handlers.

Try HEAD, fall back minimal GET HEAD rejected loses required redirect behavior. gx_jsonld() negotiates JSON-LD, inspects HTML <script type="application/ld+json"> blocks. performs standards-based expansion bundled/allowlisted contexts default; arbitrary remote context loading disabled unless explicitly enabled. extractor supports documented Geoconnex profiles known malformed variants. must handle provider object IRI, WKT string value object, nested indirect-position keys, @graph, arrays/singletons, expanded/prefixed properties, missing geometry. Unsupported contradictory values produce structured parser diagnostics; never silently discarded. Acceptance: fixtures cover least five pages three providers, current reference-gage shape, @graph, aliased terms, multiple datasets/distributions/variables, missing geometry, open temporal intervals, malformed contexts, oversized/deep documents. Build→expand→parse round trips property-tested M10.
